Package io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.transport

Class AbstractTransport

java.lang.Object
io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.transport.AbstractTransport
All Implemented Interfaces:
Transport, AutoCloseable
Direct Known Subclasses:
AbstractStreamTransport, WsTransport
public abstract class AbstractTransport extends Object implements Transport
Lifecycle skeleton shared by every Transport implementation, regardless of whether the underlying protocol is stream-oriented (TCP, TLS) or message-oriented (WebSocket).

Owns:

  • The opened / closed atomic flags and the isOpen() computation.
  • The inbound queue that receive(long, TimeUnit) polls.
  • The reader-error slot that receive(long, TimeUnit) surfaces on timeout.
  • The idempotent close() entry point.

Concrete transports implement doConnect() (open the underlying link, arrange for received batches to arrive at deliver(byte[])), doSend(byte[]) (write one batch synchronously), and doClose() (release resources; called exactly once even under concurrent close attempts).

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractTransport

      public AbstractTransport()

  • Method Details

    • doConnect

      protected abstract void doConnect() throws TransportException
      Open the underlying link. Called exactly once by connect() under the once-only guard. Subclasses arrange for received batches to be delivered via deliver(byte[]), and for asynchronous failures to be reported via reportReaderError(Throwable).
      Throws:
      TransportException - on connect failure (opened flag is cleared by connect() before rethrow)

    • doSend

      protected abstract void doSend(byte[] batch) throws TransportException
      Write one batch synchronously. Called under the caller thread; the caller does NOT hold any internal lock at this point. Subclasses may serialise on their own writer or rely on their transport primitive being thread-safe.
      Throws:
      TransportException

    • doClose

      protected abstract void doClose()
      Release resources. Called at most once; the atomic-CAS in close() guarantees no re-entrance.

    • deliver

      protected final void deliver(byte[] batch)
      Deliver a received batch to the inbox. Called from the reader thread / async callback.

    • reportReaderError

      protected final void reportReaderError(Throwable t)
      Report an asynchronous reader error. First call wins; subsequent calls are ignored. EOFException is the well-known clean-close sentinel.

    • connect

      public final void connect() throws TransportException
      Description copied from interface: Transport
      Open the link. After this returns, Transport.send(byte[]) and Transport.receive(long, TimeUnit) become usable. May only be called once per instance.
      Specified by:
      connect in interface Transport
      Throws:
      TransportException - on connect failure

    • send

      public final void send(byte[] batch) throws TransportException
      Description copied from interface: Transport
      Enqueue one batch of bytes to be written to the peer. Blocks until the batch has been fully handed to the OS write buffer.
      Specified by:
      send in interface Transport
      Throws:
      TransportException - if the link is closed, or the write fails, or the batch exceeds the transport's frame cap

    • receive

      public final byte[] receive(long timeout, TimeUnit unit) throws TransportException, InterruptedException
      Description copied from interface: Transport
      Take the next received batch from the reader inbox. Blocks up to the given timeout.
      Specified by:
      receive in interface Transport
      Returns:
      the next batch, or null if the timeout elapsed with no batch available
      Throws:
      TransportException - if the reader ended with an error (peer close is signalled by returning null and Transport.isOpen() transitioning to false)
      InterruptedException - if the caller thread was interrupted

    • isOpen

      public final boolean isOpen()
      Description copied from interface: Transport
      true between successful Transport.connect() and any close (clean or dirty).
      Specified by:
      isOpen in interface Transport

    • close

      public final void close()
      Description copied from interface: Transport
      Close the link. Idempotent: multiple calls are legal and only the first has effect. Unblocks any in-flight Transport.receive(long, TimeUnit) caller (with null for clean shutdown, or by propagating the underlying error for dirty).
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface Transport
